title: Delissea tritomantha
intro: Delissea tritomantha, also known as Hawaiian heath, is a rare and endemic flowering plant native to the islands of Hawaii. The health benefits of Delissea tritomantha include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, colds, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Delissea tritomantha include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Delissea tritomantha is classified within the family Campanulaceae, commonly known as the bellflower family. This family is a part of the larger group Campanulales, an order of flowering plants. Campanulales belongs to the clade called asterids, which also includes the orders Asterales and Escalloniales. The clade asterids is part of the larger group called core eudicots, a subgroup within the angiosperms. Core eudicots are further classified within the larger group called the eudicots.
